response: {"purpose":"Figure 4a displays the distribution of RBFOX2 eCLIP-identified clusters split by whether they are enriched or depleted relative to the paired size-matched input (SMInput), illustrating how the SMInput normalization strategy distinguishes true protein-binding signal from background. This comparison matters because abundant noncoding RNAs can dominate sequencing reads in CLIP experiments, and the SMInput control allows researchers to determine whether clusters on such RNAs reflect genuine RBFOX2 binding or simply reflect RNA abundance. By quantifying the proportion of enriched versus depleted clusters across genomic regions, the panel demonstrates the specificity and sensitivity gains afforded by the eCLIP approach over earlier CLIP methods.","title":"RBFOX2 eCLIP Cluster Enrichment vs. Depletion Relative to SMInput"}
